_notes: The trust boundary for AI-initiated repair actions. Bind-mounted into the blox-ai container as /etc/fula/action_whitelist.json:ro.
Phase 10 ships the executor that ENFORCES this whitelist. Phase 7 just defines the boundary.

Action names align with the dispatch keys in docker/fxsupport/linux/local_command_server.py wherever possible (per advisor consensus) so the executor does not need a translation table. Entries with `maps_to_core: true` are dispatched by writing to /home/pi/commands/.command_<name> flag files (commands.sh / local_command_server.py picks them up). Entries with `maps_to_core: false` are container-side (parameterized actions whose vocabulary core does not have a single entry for).
AI may never invoke an action name not in tier_1_read / tier_2_idempotent / tier_3_destructive. Fail-closed at the executor.

tier_1_read._notes: Side-effect-free probes. AI runs without approval. diag/* is the namespace shipped in Phase 6's ble_commands.json.

tier_2_idempotent._notes: Idempotent repairs the user approves with a single tap (no security code).

docker.restart.description: Restart one container in the fula stack. Arg-constrained.

systemctl.restart.description: Restart one host unit from the canonical fula set. Arg-constrained.

systemctl.reset-failed.description: Clear systemd start-limit lockout for one fula unit. Arg-constrained.

wireguard.bounce.description: wg-quick down support && wg-quick up support

ntp.resync.description: Force chrony makestep / timesyncd restart per detected daemon

tier_3_destructive._notes: Destructive or hard-to-reverse. User must enter security code + press-and-hold (Phase 12 UX).

reset.description: Full system reset — deletes config and reboots. NOT a plain reboot.

partition.description: Expand uniondrive partition (typically only useful at provisioning).

node_delete.description: Purge chain DB (forces re-sync; minutes to hours).

force_update.description: Force OTA pull + restart of fula stack.

argument_constraints._notes: Even if action_name is whitelisted, the args must match. Fail-closed at executor. NOT applicable to maps_to_core actions — those take no args and dispatch via flag files.

docker.restart._notes: ipfs_host (kubo) is deliberately EXCLUDED. Kubo is the base IPFS daemon that ipfs_cluster, fula_go, fula_pinning and fula_gateway all depend on; restarting it alone leaves them attached to a daemon that vanished underneath them. Any kubo-level repair must use restart_fula, which bounces the whole stack in dependency order. Enforced here (not just in the trees/runbook) so even the free-form LLM path cannot single-restart kubo — the executor fail-closes on container=ipfs_host. ipfs_cluster and fula_go remain individually restartable because they reconnect to a still-running kubo.

systemctl.reset-failed._notes: Includes fula-readiness-check-recover.service (not in systemctl.restart above) because recover.service is Type=oneshot — restarting a oneshot is meaningless, but reset-failed clears the start-limit lockout the recover unit can hit. See Phase 2 work.
